steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what an energy ladder model advocate would say explains Brazil's shift away from solid fuels. The passage defines the model: fuel choice is driven by household income, with high-technology fuels displacing solid fuels as incomes rise.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Between 2000 and 2019, household incomes rose in Brazil, resulting in households meeting a greater proportion of their fuel needs with high-technology fuels.: directly applies the model's core mechanism. ✓
  • Choice B — Beginning in 2000, high-technology fuels became more readily available in Brazil, enabling households of various income levels to shift away from solid fuels.: cites supply, not income — a different causal factor the model doesn't use. ✗
  • Choice C — Solid fuel use declined in Brazil between 2000 and 2019 because changes in household income during that period made solid fuels more expensive relative to income.: pricing is outside the income-mediated framework the model describes. ✗
  • Choice D — Household incomes in Brazil rose from 2000 to 2019, which weakened the association between income and household fuel choice.: contradicts the model rather than supporting it. ✗
A is correct.
